acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] PeerAckTable stores per-peer durable/applied watermarks and heads with monotonic updates.
- [ ] Divergent head sha at same seq is detected and excluded from eligibility.
- [ ] satisfied_k computes k-of-n for eligible peers with explicit failure cases.
- [ ] Unit tests cover monotonic updates and satisfied_k behavior.
description:
**Problem**
ReplicatedFsync(k) requires a truthful view of which peers have durably ACKed which watermarks and head hashes. A naive map risks mixing namespaces or ignoring head hash divergence.

**Context**
- REALTIME_PLAN.md §0.12 (applied vs durable + head sha), §10.1-10.3 (durability coordination), §9.5 (ACK semantics)
- Stateright model: beads_stateright_models/src/realtime_types_sketch.rs (Watermarks<Durable>, HeadStatus)

**Files:** src/daemon/repl/peer_acks.rs (new), src/daemon/durability_coordinator.rs, src/daemon/store_runtime.rs
design:
**Design**
- PeerAckTable tracks per peer: durable Watermarks<Durable>, optional durable_heads, and last_ack_at_ms.
- Enforce monotonicity on update: seq may only advance; head sha must match the seq (reject divergence).
- Provide queries: acked_by(ns, origin, seq) -> Vec<ReplicaId>, and satisfied_k(ns, origin, seq, k) -> bool.
- Maintain per-namespace eligibility via roster (durability_eligible); only count eligible peers.
- Surface divergence: if a peer reports the same seq but a different head sha, mark that peer as diverged and exclude from durability quorum.
title: Phase 5: PeerAckTable for durability coordination

description: StatusCollector and ReplRig admin_status open a fresh IPC connection per sample; polling loops can churn sockets. Add a persistent IpcConnection (or a test-only IPC session helper) to reuse the connection for repeated status queries.
title: StatusCollector/admin_status polls open new IPC connection each time

description:
**Problem**
CLI handlers perform read-modify-write for labels and parent relationships. `src/cli/commands/label.rs` fetches each issue then sends a full `Update` patch, and `src/cli/commands/update.rs` queries deps then removes/adds parent edges. This makes changes non-atomic, adds round trips, and splits core behavior across CLI and daemon.

**Design**
Add daemon-side ops for label and parent mutations and switch CLI to use them:
- Extend IPC `Request` with `AddLabels { repo, id, labels: Vec<String> }` and `RemoveLabels { repo, id, labels: Vec<String> }`, or a single `UpdateLabels { add, remove }` request.
- Add `SetParent { repo, id, parent: Option<String> }` that replaces existing parent edges in one mutation.
- Implement handlers in `src/daemon/executor.rs` that apply the changes atomically in a single WAL mutation, validating label formats and parent existence.
- Update CLI `label` commands to call the new label ops; update `update` to call `SetParent` when `--parent/--no-parent` is provided.
- Keep response payloads consistent (use `OpResult::Updated` or add a small label/parent result if needed for clarity).

**Design Notes**
This keeps CLI thin and centralizes behavior in the daemon. It also avoids subtle races (labels changed between fetch and update) and makes parent updates atomic.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] CLI label add/remove no longer fetches issues; daemon applies label changes atomically.
- [ ] Parent updates are handled by a dedicated daemon op; CLI no longer manipulates deps directly.
- [ ] Label validation errors and missing parent errors remain user-friendly.
- [ ] Run `codex review --uncommitted` (timeout 10m) and address findings.
- [ ] Tests pass.

**Files:** `src/daemon/ipc.rs`, `src/daemon/ops.rs`, `src/daemon/executor.rs`, `src/cli/commands/label.rs`, `src/cli/commands/update.rs`, `src/api/mod.rs`
title: Move label/parent updates into daemon ops
crash_recovery_rebuilds_index_after_fsync_before_commit fails: wal hang marker missing at crash_recovery.rs:242

acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Child beads exist for each execution phase with grounded context and file lists
- [ ] Dependency ordering is encoded in the tracker
- [ ] The work can be executed from the tracker plus repo-local spec/plan without chat history
- [ ] Full closeout criteria are captured in the final verification bead
description:
**Problem**
The workspace split landed, but the final public boundaries are still leaky. `beads-rs` still reaches into `beads_daemon::runtime::*`, `beads-daemon` still exposes `runtime` and re-exports `beads-git`, path/config/repo bootstrap ownership is duplicated across crates, and `beads-cli` still owns some package/bootstrap concerns that are not really CLI-language concerns.

**Goal**
Finish the hard-cutover cleanup so the codebase has genuinely local crate reasoning:
- `beads-rs` becomes a tiny assembly crate
- `beads-daemon` exposes only deliberate top-level public APIs
- `beads-daemon::runtime::*` is no longer a public dependency path
- `beads-daemon::git::*` disappears entirely
- shared bootstrap ownership moves into a dedicated `beads-bootstrap` crate
- git-owned store/state loading moves into `beads-git`
- `beads-cli` narrows to command language + presentation, not bootstrap/package policy

**Context**
This epic is the follow-on to the earlier crate split work (`bd-14fs`, `bd-21eg`, `bd-6ea1`). Those efforts created the workspace DAG and moved the big subsystems into dedicated crates. This epic is for the final sealing and ownership cleanup.

**References**
- Spec: `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-03-13-final-crate-boundary-cleanup-design.md`
- Plan: `docs/superpowers/plans/2026-03-13-final-crate-boundary-cleanup.md`
- DAG policy: `docs/CRATE_DAG.md`
- Current coupling inventory: `docs/architecture/non-cli-daemon-coupling-inventory.md`

**Definition of Done**
The epic is complete when:
- no external crate imports `beads_daemon::runtime::*`
- no code imports `beads_daemon::git::*`
- bootstrap ownership (config/paths/repo-root discovery) has one source of truth
- git-owned store/state loading lives in `beads-git`
- `beads-rs` reads as assembly-only when opened cold
- `beads-cli` is limited to the `bd` command language and presentation layer
- package-level and workspace-level verification gates pass

**Files to Study**
- `crates/beads-daemon/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/beads-rs/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/beads-rs/src/store_admin.rs`
- `crates/beads-rs/src/upgrade.rs`
- `crates/beads-rs/src/paths.rs`
- `crates/beads-rs/src/repo.rs`
- `crates/beads-rs/src/config/**`
- `crates/beads-daemon/src/config.rs`
- `crates/beads-daemon/src/paths.rs`
- `crates/beads-daemon/src/repo.rs`
- `crates/beads-cli/src/paths.rs`
- `crates/beads-cli/src/upgrade/**`
- `crates/beads-git/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/beads-git/src/paths.rs`
design:
Drive the cleanup in ordered phases:
1. Seal daemon public boundaries and remove runtime/git reach-throughs.
2. Create `beads-bootstrap` and move pure bootstrap ownership there.
3. Re-home git-owned helpers into `beads-git`.
4. Narrow `beads-rs` and `beads-cli` to their real roles.
5. Re-home tests and close with docs + verification.

Do not introduce compatibility shims as durable architecture. Short-lived branch-local shims are acceptable only if they are removed before closeout.

notes.author: darin@book
notes.content:
Epic closeout audit on 2026-03-13 from /Users/darin/src/personal/beads-rs.

The hard-cutover is complete. `beads-core`, `beads-bootstrap`, `beads-git`, `beads-daemon-core`, `beads-daemon`, `beads-cli`, and `beads-rs` now have owner-aligned boundaries; `beads-rs` reads as an assembly crate; daemon runtime/git reach-throughs are gone; bootstrap ownership is centralized; git-owned state loading moved into `beads-git`; and owner tests were re-homed out of `beads-rs`.

Closeout evidence:
- docs/spec/plan refreshed and aligned (`ab7a92ae`)
- clippy/dead-surface cleanup landed (`4dbf5b8c`, `dc435d3d`)
- daemon test/cache isolation fix landed (`42f4dc50`)
- adversarial review completed; only non-blocking residual is tracked as `beads-rs-vi7x`
- historical stale-open epics `bd-14fs` and `bd-6ea1` were re-audited and closed so tracker state matches reality

Final verification on current stack:
- `cargo fmt --all --check`
- `just dylint`
- `cargo clippy --all-features -- -D warnings`
- `cargo test`
- `cargo test --features slow-tests`
- `cargo check -p beads-rs --all-features`

title: Hard-cutover final crate boundary cleanup and bootstrap extraction
